title: Repeatable Adobe pipeline turning one raw exercise clip into a branded vertical coaching reel (subtitles cue, cleaned VO, watermark, muscle-group callout)
one_line_ask: Run our repeatable exercise-reel pipeline on one raw 'Goblet Squat' clip: summarize it for subtitle/cue text and naming, clean the coaching voiceover, build the reusable white-watermark and branded muscle-group arrow overlays, reframe to vertical, cut a 30s reference reel, and file everything under our naming convention.

issues: Slight luminance/texture variation in the red fill (acceptable marker texture)
Arrow tail label box is small and slightly low contrast but legible
Curve of arrow is slightly asymmetric but consistent with hand-drawn style
Slight paper texture visible (acceptable per brief, but not perfectly flat vector)
Very subtle shadow/drop effect around the square (not a pure digital flat background)

Monogram mark reads ambiguously — resembles 'HEH' or a stylised barbell icon rather than a clearly intentional letterform monogram; the geometric construction is slightly inconsistent (left and right barbell collar elements differ subtly in weight)

Background is a very light grey which is acceptable but the card/square is presented as a slight 3D mockup with drop shadow and perspective tilt — adds mild CGI/mockup quality rather than a flat source file feel, though within tolerable range
Wordmark is split across two lines (IRONLINE / COACHING) which may or may not match brief intent — no explicit line-break instruction given
Minor soft edge on the card corners due to mockup rendering

assets.name: goblet_squat_raw_4k.mp4

issues: Slight motion blur on dumbbell hand consistent with movement - acceptable for raw video
Squat depth not fully visible due to crop - minor framing note

qc.transcript: Chest up. Weight in the heels. Drive to the floor.

assets.note: Representative ~8s Veo-3.1 clip WITH NATIVE AUDIO: the coach demonstrates a goblet squat (single dumbbell held at the chest) and speaks audible English form cues with slight gym-mic reverb ('Chest up. Weight in the heels. Drive to the floor.') so the downstream media_summarize (transcribe) and media_enhance_speech (de-echo) connector steps have real speech to operate on. Regenerated by independent audit because the prior Seedance take was silent, violating the spec's 'Native audio required'. The brief's raw clip is ~45s; length is not load-bearing (task reframes/cuts it). Audio verified by Whisper transcription; frames verified brand-clean.
